content.full_text:
Re
Feln Polrrrcrl Pnecrrcss C oulrrssroN
PO. Bor 307 . +18 J Srreer . Sacmrncnro. CA g-5Sll-()1t07
(916) lll-566() . Fax (9t6) 3t2-0tj,!6
October 23,2000
David E. McCauley
City of La Habra
P.O. Box 337
210 E. La Habra Blvd.
La Habra, CA 90633-0337
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-00-219
Dear Mr. McCauley:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("the Act").1 Since your request seeks ttrira party a-dvice and advice
based on past conduct, we cannot provide formal written advice. (Regulation 18329.)
QUESTIONS
What reporting obligations, if any, are triggered for city council candidate
McCauley by voluntarily hosting a local cable television show sponsored by the La
Habra Chamber of Commerce (the "Chamber"); and does the Act require the Chamber or
Adelphia Cable to provide equal airtime to other candidates for city council?
CONCLUSION
Since your questions pertain in large part to past conduct, the Commission cannot
provide formal written advice; however, we have enclosed general information regarding
express advocacy and reporting requirements.'
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections 18109-
18996, of the California Code of Regulations. B"caus" your-ietter pr"r"rrtt a question based primarily on
past conduct, we are heating it as a request for informal assistance pursuant to regulation 183)9(c).
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83 I 14; regulation 18329(c)(3).)
2 Copies ofRegulations 18215 and18225 are enclosed.


File No. I-00-219
Page No. 2
FACTS
You were appointed to the La Habra City Council in February 2000 and are
running for election to the same office in November. You have been hosting a cable
television show, "The La Habra Connection," three times a week on a voluntary basis.3
The show is a one half-hour program about the City of La Habra, its history, current
activities, local businesses and upcoming events. No reference to your candidacy for citv
council has been mentioned on the show. The program is sponsored by the Chamber of
Commerce and produced by Adelphia Cable.
During our October 2 phone conversation, you advised me that you have
requested Adelphia to discontinue airing the show until after the election, to avoid any
app earance o f impropriety.
You would like to know whether hosting this cable show has triggered any
reporting obligations for you, and also whether the Chamber or Adelphia must provide
equal airtime for the other council candidates.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires disclosure of contributions received and expenditures made by
campaign committees. Section 82013 defines a "committee" as:
"[A]ny person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly
does any of the following:
(a) 
Receives contributions totaling one thousand dollars
($1,000) or more in a calendar year.
(b) 
Makes independent expenditures totaling one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or
(c) 
Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars ($10,000)
or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or
committees."
candidate controlled committees are formed pursuant to subdivision (a).
Section 82015 provides that an expenditure made at the behest of a candidate is a
contribution to the candidate unless full and adequate consideration is received for
making the expenditure.4 Regulation 18215 excludes from the definition of
"contribution" a payment made at the behest of a candidate, which is for a
communication by the candidate or any other person, if the communication:
3 The video cassette containing a segment of La Habra Connection you submitted was not viewed for
purposes ofdrafting this response
" "'Made at the behest' of means made under the contol or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with, at the request or suggestion of, or with the express, prioi consent of."
(Regulation I 8225 .7 (a).)


File No. I-00-219
Page No. 3
"(i) Does not contain express advocacy;
(ii) Does not make reference to the candidate's candidacy for
elective office, the candidate's election campaign, or the
candidate's or his or her opponent's qualifications for office; and
(iii) Does not solicit contributions to the candidate or to third
persons for use in support of the candidate or in opposition to the
candidate's opponent." (Regulation 1 82 1 5(c)(a).)
A communication expressly advocates the nomination, election or defeat of a
candidate if it contains express words of advocacy such as o'vote for," "elect," "support,"
"cast your ballot," "vote against," "defeat," "reject," "sign petitions for" or otherwise
refers to a clearly identified candidate so that the communication, taken as a whole,
unambiguously urges a particular result in an election.s lRegulation 18225(bXlXDX2).)
You stated in your letter that "The La Habra Connection" television programs that
have already been taped do not reference your candidacy or qualifications for office in an
attempt to influence the voters. In addition, you have recently contacted Adelphia cable
and requested that the program be discontinued until after the election. As long as the
cable television shows do not expressly advocate either your own nomination or election,
or the defeat of your opponents, then the progrirm will not be considered a contribution or
any other type of reportable expenditure.
You also asked whether the Chamber or Adelphia must provide equal airtime for
other council candidates. The Commission provides advice to persons with duties and
responsibilities under the Act and to their authorized representatives. We cannot provide
advice to third parties about another person's duties or obligations under the Act, unless
the person whose duties or obligations are in question has authorizedthe third parry to
request advice. (Section 83114; Regulation t8329.) For this reason, we decline to
provide specific information regarding the duties or obligations of the Chamber or
Adelphia Cable.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
rA=
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosures
5 The leading 9ff Circuit authority on whether a communication, taken as a whole, unambiguously urges a
particular result in an election is Federal Elections Commission v. Furgatch (1987) 807 F.2d 857.
By
fiUqorrt t6,*,rb
Marg$et E. Fige(fid
